revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:阿刀大足]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0750|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}}}
'''Ato no Ōtari''' (阿刀 大足) was a {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|qid=Q134737|lt=noble|1=Nobility}} and {{ill|Scholar|en|Scholar|qid=Q20826540|lt=scholar|1=Scholar}} active from the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} to the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. He was the maternal uncle of {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|qid=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}}. His {{ill|court Rank|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Court Ranks in Japan|qid=Q99196082}} was {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. == Career == In the 7th year of {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|qid=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} (788), {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|qid=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}} traveled to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|en|Heijō-kyō|qid=Q862509|lt=Heijō-kyō|1=Heijō-kyō}} to seek guidance from his maternal uncle, Ōtari, who was a scholar. Until Kūkai entered the {{ill|Daigaku-ryō|en|Daigaku-ryō|qid=Q3012048|lt=Daigaku-ryō|1=Daigaku-ryō}} in Enryaku 11 (792), Ōtari personally instructed him in the {{ill|Analects|en|Analects|qid=Q276015|lt=Analects|1=Analects}}, the {{ill|Classic of Filial Piety|en|Classic of Filial Piety|qid=Q1368044|lt=Classic of Filial Piety|1=Classic of Filial Piety}}, historical texts, and {{ill|kidentō|ja|紀伝道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kidentō|1=Kidendō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11604971}}, a field of classical Chinese literature <ref>Introduction to *Sanjūshiki* (三教指帰).</ref>. In Enryaku 23 (804), thanks to Ōtari’s financial and logistical support, Kūkai was able to travel to {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|qid=Q9683|lt=Tang China|1=Tang dynasty}} as a {{ill|Gakushō|ja|学問僧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the 18th {{ill|Japanese missions to Tang China|en|Japanese missions to Tang China|qid=Q262529|lt=Japanese missions to Tang China|1=Japanese missions to Tang China}}. Additionally, Ōtari served as a {{ill|tutor|ja|侍読|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tutor|1=imperial tutor|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11385487}} to {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}, but his later movements remain unclear. It is possible that he was implicated in the {{ill|Iyo Prince Incident|ja|伊予親王の変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Prince Incident|1=incident of Imperial Prince Iyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11378832}} in Daidō 2 (807) and consequently fell from grace. His highest recorded court Rank was {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} <ref>*Shoku Nihon Kōki* (続日本後紀), Jōwa 2 (835), 3rd month, 25th day.</ref>. == References == {{reflist|2|qq=}} == Bibliography == * *Asahi Nihon Rekishi Jinbutsu Jiten* (朝日日本歴史人物事典), Asahi Shimbunsha, 1994. * {{ill|Morita Tsutomu|ja|森田悌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Tsutomu|1=Tei Morita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11540090}}, *Shoku Nihon Kōki (Volume 1)* (続日本後紀（上）), Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko, 2010. * {{ill|Toshio Hōga|lt=Toshio Hōga|lt=Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōga Toshio|qq=}}, *Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei* (古代氏族系譜集成), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986.
{{translated page|ja|阿刀大足|version=94248301|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:阿刀大足]]
